svg.title: How diffusion models work — step by step
svg.desc: A visual walkthrough of the forward (noising) and reverse (denoising) processes in a diffusion model, with training and generation phases.
svg.text: Start with a real image x₀. At each timestep t, add a small amount of Gaussian noise.
Formula: xₜ = √ᾱₜ · x₀ + √(1−ᾱₜ) · ε , ε ~ N(0, I) — one-step closed form (no loop needed!)
A UNet (or Transformer) learns to predict the noise ε that was added, given xₜ and timestep t.
Key insight: the network never sees the final answer — it only learns to guess the noise at each step t ∈ {1…T}
Start from pure Gaussian noise. Iteratively remove noise using the trained network.
Each step: xₜ₋₁ = (1/√αₜ)(xₜ − ((1−αₜ)/√(1−ᾱₜ))·ε_θ(xₜ,t)) + σₜ·z , z ~ N(0,I)
A text prompt is encoded and injected into the UNet at every denoising step via cross-attention.
Latent diffusion — why it's fast (Stable Diffusion trick)
Run diffusion in a compressed VAE latent space, not pixel space. Up to 8× cheaper!
Full pipeline: prompt → CLIP → latent noise → 20–50 denoising steps → VAE decode → image
